spec_match.rationale: The demo reads PerformanceResourceTiming resource entries, uses contentType as the primary grouping key, and treats empty contentType values as an explicit unknown bucket instead of URL guessing.

interactive.rationale: The dashboard now has dataset, grouping, metric, duration-threshold, cache-inclusion, capture, filter, and same-origin probe controls that all change the rendered chart and table.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still delivers the feature-index promise of a content-type-aware RUM dashboard with counts, transfer totals, individual rows, filtering, and production-style summary stats.

edge_cases.rationale: Edge-case mode covers empty contentType values, cached zero-byte entries, mislabelled resources, unsupported-browser fallback, and no-row filter states without throwing.

references.rationale: The page includes the required chromestatus.com/feature/5156068351541248 link and a Resource Timing contentType spec link.

design_system.rationale: Inline styles use the shared CSS variables and color-mix expressions, local button backgrounds also set color, and no raw hex colors remain in the owned concept page.

summary: Goal-setting pass resolved the previous major ChromeStatus reference gap and the related moderate interaction/edge-case concerns. Browser QA on localhost exercised edge-case mode, filtering, metric changes, live same-origin probes, and console health successfully.
